science.title: Scientists catch antimatter “atom” acting like a wave for the first time
science.summary: Quantum physics once shocked scientists by revealing that particles can behave like waves—and now, that strange behavior has been pushed even further. For the first time, researchers have observed wave-like interference in positronium, an exotic “atom” made of an electron and its antimatter partner, a positron. This br

science.title: Scientists just captured a mysterious quantum “dance” inside superconductors
science.summary: In a breakthrough experiment, scientists directly imaged how particles pair up in a system that mimics superconductors. Instead of behaving independently, the pairs moved in a synchronized, dance-like pattern—something never predicted before. This suggests a major gap in the classic theory of superconductivity.

science.title: Mezcal worm in a bottle DNA test reveals a surprise
science.summary: The famous mezcal “worm” has long puzzled scientists, but DNA testing has finally cracked the case. Researchers found that all sampled larvae were actually agave redworm moth caterpillars—not a mix of species as once believed. While the discovery clears up a long-standing mystery, it also raises concerns about sustaina

science.title: Microscopic sensors uncover how liquids turn glassy without structural change
science.summary: A scientific discovery by researchers at Tel Aviv University's School of Chemistry offers a new perspective on a long-standing scientific mystery: how does a flowing liquid suddenly become a rigid, almost frozen material, without changing its structure? This phenomenon, known as the "glass transition," has puzzled phys

science.title: Breakthrough Bowel Cancer Trial Leaves Patients Cancer-Free for Nearly 3 Years
science.summary: A new approach to treating certain bowel cancers is showing long-lasting effects. Patients with a particular form of bowel cancer have remained cancer-free for nearly three years after receiving a short course of immunotherapy before surgery, rather than chemotherapy afterward. These findings come from the NEOPRISM-CRC

tech.title: The Download: DeepSeek’s latest AI breakthrough, and the race to build world models
tech.summary: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Three reasons why DeepSeek’s new model matters On Friday, Chinese AI firm DeepSeek released a preview of V4, its long-awaited new flagship model. Notably, the model can p

tech.title: Humanoid robots start sorting luggage in Tokyo airport test amid labor shortage
tech.summary: Humanoid robots could load cargo and clean aircraft cabins at Haneda Airport.

tech.title: Fusion power startup Zap Energy pulls a partial pivot, adding nuclear fission to the mix
tech.summary: Surprise! Fusion startup Zap Energy says it will be developing fission reactors alongside its fusion devices.

tech.title: Google Photos uses AI to make the iconic closet from ‘Clueless’ a reality
tech.summary: Google says the new feature will leverage AI technology to automatically create a copy of your wardrobe that's based on the pieces of clothing appearing in your Google Photos library.

tech.title: BMW i Ventures has a new $300M fund and AI is riding shotgun
tech.summary: BMW i Ventures is interested in startups working on agentic AI and physical AI, as well as industrial software, advanced materials, and manufacturing and supply chain technologies.

medicine.title: Preclinical efficacy of experimental peptide therapy suggests a new target for metastatic breast cancer treatments
medicine.summary: A peptide that successfully targeted and killed metastatic cancer cells in mice may open the door to developing more efficient ways to detect and treat metastatic breast cancer in humans, UTHealth Houston researchers have discovered. The research was published in Molecular Therapy Oncology and led by Mikhail Kolonin, P

medicine.title: Why CAR T therapy works for some patients but fails for others may be getting clearer
medicine.summary: Chimeric antigen receptor (CAR) T-cell therapy is one of oncology's most powerful ideas: Harvest a cancer patient's own immune cells, genetically engineer them to recognize tumor cells, multiply them in a laboratory and reinject them as a living drug. For some patients, the treatment produces extraordinary, durable rem

medicine.title: Integrating pharmacists into kidney disease care team can provide better outcomes for patients
medicine.summary: Approximately 35 million adults in the United States suffer from chronic kidney disease (CKD), and that number is growing. Meanwhile, there's a critical shortage of nephrologists to manage these patients, according to Calvin Meaney, PharmD, clinical associate professor of pharmacy practice who specializes in nephrology

medicine.title: A forgotten drug is giving new hope to kids with a rare disease
medicine.summary: A decades-old drug once used to treat sleeping sickness is now showing surprising promise against an ultra-rare and life-threatening genetic disorder called Bachmann-Bupp syndrome (BABS). Early patient treatments suggest the drug, DFMO, may ease severe symptoms by targeting the underlying genetic malfunction. Researche

medicine.title: Simple “gut reset” may stop weight gain after Ozempic or Wegovy
medicine.summary: A new minimally invasive procedure may help people keep weight off after stopping popular drugs like Ozempic and semaglutide—something most patients struggle with. In a clinical trial, those who underwent a technique called duodenal mucosal resurfacing regained far less weight compared to others after discontinuing the

mathematics.title: In Math, Rigor Is Vital. But Are Digitized Proofs Taking It Too Far?
mathematics.summary: The quest to make mathematics rigorous has a long and spotty history — one mathematicians can learn from as they push to formalize everything in the computer program Lean. The post In Math, Rigor Is Vital. But Are Digitized Proofs Taking It Too Far? first appeared on Quanta Magazine

mathematics.title: MIT affiliates win AI for Math grants to accelerate mathematical discovery
mathematics.summary: Department of Mathematics researchers David Roe and Andrew Sutherland seek to advance automated theorem proving; four additional MIT alumni also awarded.

mathematics.title: School of Science welcomed new faculty in 2024
mathematics.summary: Eleven new professors join the departments of Biology; Brain and Cognitive Sciences; Earth, Atmospheric and Planetary Sciences; Mathematics; and Physics.

mathematics.title: Laurent Demanet appointed co-director of MIT Center for Computational Science and Engineering
mathematics.summary: Applied mathematics professor will join fellow co-director Nicolas Hadjiconstantinou in leading the cross-cutting center.

mathematics.title: Why Math’s Final Axiom Proved So Controversial
mathematics.summary: Zermelo-Fraenkel set theory is so widely accepted that modern mathematicians hardly think about it. But believing in its core principles didn’t come easily. The post Why Math’s Final Axiom Proved So Controversial first appeared on Quanta Magazine

space.title: SpaceX launches its 50th mission of the year, sends 25 Starlink satellites to orbit (video)
space.summary: A SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket launched 25 Starlink internet satellites to orbit from California on Sunday (April 26). It was the company's 50th liftoff of the year already.

space.title: SpaceX rocket debris could slam into the moon: Here's what you need to know
space.summary: Earth's moon is to be on the receiving end of a spent rocket stage in early August - the leftovers from a SpaceX Falcon 9 launch last year.

space.title: SpaceX Falcon Heavy rocket lifts off on 1st launch in 18 months
space.summary: SpaceX launched its Falcon Heavy rocket for the first time in 18 months on Wednesday morning (April 29), delivering the ViaSat-3 F3 communications satellite into geostationary orbit.

space.title: The 'Oscars of Science': Breakthrough Prize 2026 awards over $18 million for discoveries across space, physics and more
space.summary: The 2026 Breakthrough Prize honored advances in dark matter, quantum physics, gene editing and nonlinear mathematics.

space.title: Trump invited the Artemis 2 moon astronauts to the Oval Office. Here's what happened
space.summary: President Trump hosted the Artemis 2 moon astronauts at the White House today (April 29), congratulating the quartet and musing about the possibility of going to space himself.

astronomy.title: Binary Stars Form Lots Of Exoplanets, But Many Of Them Are Ejected As Rogue Planets
astronomy.summary: Binary stars are common, but for a long time astronomers have thought that exoplanets would have trouble forming around them. In recent years, powerful telescopes have detected about 50 of these planets. Now, new simulations show that their formation isn't actually rare, it's just that they tend to be on wide orbits, w

astronomy.title: Could Light Alone Get Us to Another Star?
astronomy.summary: Using nothing but a laser beam, scientists at Texas A&M University have demonstrated that tiny engineered devices can be lifted and steered in three dimensions without any physical contact. This breakthrough could one day form the basis of a propulsion system capable of reaching our nearest neighbouring stars in de

astronomy.title: Catching the Earliest Stars in the Universe
astronomy.summary: New observations from the James Webb Space Telescope might have caught the signature of the universe's first stars. The post Catching the Earliest Stars in the Universe appeared first on Sky & Telescope.

astronomy.title: Astronomers Find the Edge of the Milky Way
astronomy.summary: Astronomers have located the edge of the Milky Way’s star-forming disk for the first time, showing that star formation is focused within 40,000 light-years of our galactic center. The post Astronomers Find the Edge of the Milky Way appeared first on Sky & Telescope.

astronomy.title: Canada Proposes POET Mission to Hunt Earth-Sized Planets
astronomy.summary: Exoplanet science and the search for life beyond Earth continue to advance at break-neck speeds, with the number of confirmed exoplanets by NASA rapidly approaching 6,300, with 223 of those exoplanets being designated as terrestrial (rocky) exoplanets. With the promise of discovering an increasing number of Earth-sized

architecture.title: SOM "preserves the soul" of 1960s modernist office complex in Milan
architecture.summary: Global studio SOM has renewed Corso Italia 23, a 1960s office complex in Milan, retrofitting its modernist structure and fractured interiors to align with modern standards. Occupying an entire urban block, the Corso Italia 23 building comprises three distinct volumes originally designed by Italian architects Gio Ponti

architecture.title: Diamond Schmitt and MVRDV reveal design of terraced research building for University of Toronto
architecture.summary: Architecture studios Diamond Schmitt, MVRDV and Two Row Architect have revealed the design for the Temerty Building at the University of Toronto, a research building informed by Indigenous design principles. The building will be located on the edge of a campus lawn at the University of Toronto St George campus and host

architecture.title: Gensler set to design Trump Tower Tbilisi as country's tallest building
architecture.summary: The Trump Organization has announced plans to build a 70-storey skyscraper in Tbilisi, Georgia, which would be the tallest building in the country. Planned in collaboration with Biograpi Living, Archi Group, The Sapir Organization, Blox Group and Finvest Georgia, the skyscraper is set to be designed by US architecture

architecture.title: the city as a controlled hallucination: from amusement parks to urban space
architecture.summary: before it is built, space is tested through illusion and movement, shaping how it is navigated and understood. The post the city as a controlled hallucination: from amusement parks to urban space appeared first on designboom | architecture & design magazine.

architecture.title: Minoru Yamasaki's Northwestern National Life Building in Minneapolis to Be Converted Into Hotel
architecture.summary: A building designed by Minoru Yamasaki in downtown Minneapolis is set to be converted into a hotel, marking a new phase in the life of the former headquarters of the Northwestern National Life Insurance Company, one of the architect's lesser-known yet formally distinctive works. Vacant since 2023, the building at 20 Wa

construction.title: Granite scoops up Utah infrastructure contractor
construction.summary: The California-based firm bought Kenny Seng Construction, which focuses on earthwork and site preparation as well as managing a gravel pit and recycling yard.

construction.title: Kiewit ‘off-ramped’ from Baltimore’s Key Bridge rebuild
construction.summary: The Maryland Transportation Authority informed the firm Tuesday that it would not be retained for the next phase of the project’s construction, which has seen its cost balloon.

construction.title: Letter to the Editor: There's Nothing New Under the Sun for Bridges
construction.summary: I get a bit amused by articles on “new” construction methods for bridges, such as the following one in Ohio.

construction.title: Correction: Wrong Casino Collapse Photo
construction.summary: Related Links: Engineering News Record Architectural Record ﻿The story "OSHA Points to Process Errors in Cincinnati Casino Collapse" contained errors. The photograph depicted another Horseshoe Casino, which collapsed last December in Cleveland. Also, Messer Construction Co. received its last OSHA violation in Ja

construction.title: US hotel construction pipeline down roughly 5% YOY in Q1 2026
construction.summary: Despite the decline, the luxury segment reached a record 102 projects in the quarter, up 16% year over year, per a Lodging Econometrics report.

ai.title: A Systematic Approach for Large Language Models Debugging
ai.summary: arXiv:2604.23027v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have become central to modern AI workflows, powering applications from open-ended text generation to complex agent-based reasoning. ai.summary: arXiv:2604.23057v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We investigate whether explicit belief graphs improve LLM performance in cooperative multi-agent reasoning. ai.summary: arXiv:2604.23072v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language model (LLM) agents are increasingly tasked with complex real-world analysis (e.g., in financial forecasting, scientific discovery), yet their reasoning suffers from stochastic instability and lacks a verifiable, compositional structure.
